name: AD Privileged Users or Groups Reconnaissance Detected
description: Detects reconnaissance of privileged users or groups in Active Directory using Event ID 4661 and objects with SIDs or names associated with sensitive roles such as Domain Admins, Enterprise Admins, KDC, Group Policy Creators, Backup Operators, etc. This is a critical indicator of an adversary performing discovery to identify high-value targets.

prompts.prompt: An alert for **Active Directory Reconnaissance** (**MITRE ATT&CK T1087**) has been triggered on the Domain Controller. The user **{subject_user_name}** attempted to access information about a highly privileged object, **{object_name}**, with the object type **{object_type}**. This activity is a strong indicator of an adversary performing **Discovery** to find accounts that can be used for privilege escalation or lateral movement. Immediate investigation is required to determine if this is a legitimate administrative action or a malicious reconnaissance attempt. The event occurred with Event ID **4661** and process name **{process_name}**.

parameters.name: subject_user_name
parameters.description: The user account that performed the reconnaissance.

parameters.name: object_name
parameters.description: The name of the privileged object that was accessed.

parameters.name: object_type
parameters.description: The type of object that was accessed (e.g., 'SAM_USER' or 'SAM_GROUP').

parameters.name: process_name
parameters.description: The process that performed the reconnaissance.

parameters.name: hostname
parameters.description: The hostname of the system where the event occurred.
